question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: RecordFile (field: sidecars, @Builder.Default=List.of())] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where sidecars is empty but sidecarCount is non-zero, causing sidecar download logic to attempt to fetch non-existent sidecar files, blocking the gossip pipeline for the file's transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: RecordFile (field: logsBloom)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile with a logsBloom byte array of zero length or incorrect length (not 256 bytes), causing a downstream bloom filter operation to throw an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException that aborts gossip of the file's transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: RecordFile (field: consensusEnd, @Id)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with consensusEnd set to 0 or a negative value, causing the Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, consensusEnd) conversion to produce an invalid timestamp that breaks latency metric recording and aborts gossip of the file's transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: RecordFile (field: hash)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a hash value that is a valid hex string but of incorrect length (not 96 hex chars for SHA-384), causing Hex.decode to throw an exception in the cutover hash chain logic and halting gossip of subsequent transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: RecordFile (field: previousHash)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a previousHash that is a valid hex string but of incorrect length, causing Hex.decode in the cutover path (blockFile.setPreviousWrappedRecordBlockHash(Hex.decode(blockFile.getPreviousHash()))) to throw an exception and halt gossip of all subsequent transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: RecordFile (field: version)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the version field to trigger the isLastRecordFile branch (last.getVersion() < BlockStreamReader.VERSION) in verifyHashChain, causing the wrong hash chain verification path to be used and allowing a file with omitted transactions to pass validation?
